extraction.one_line_summary: EDET Nikias won from a breakaway in wet conditions, sprinting in the saddle for greater traction on greasy roads in Igualada
tactical_patterns: STETINA Peter initiated early breakaway attacks on first climb but aggressive tempo prevented anyone establishing clear gap preemptively
BARCELÓ Fernando bridged solo using superior climbing skills from Contador foundation training, demonstrating opportunism when break fragmented on climb
ŠTYBAR Zdeněk exploited wet descent corners aggressively as heavy rider, negating usual weight disadvantage to gain margin into final kilometers
EDET Nicolas sprinted seated for wet road traction advantage while rivals stood, revealing superior technical adaptation under slippery conditions

rider_signals.SÁNCHEZ Luis León: neutral: positioned in breakaway but minimal work contribution, likely preserving energy while Astana controlled peloton
rider_signals.TEUNS Dylan: positive: inserted into break day after losing red jersey from 18th at 8:44, demonstrated immediate recovery and opportunistic mindset
rider_signals.DE LA CRUZ David: positive: local Catalan rider swept wide on final corners for speed advantage, nearly won from break despite wet conditions
rider_signals.BARCELÓ Fernando: positive: soloed across to leaders using climbing ability, showed initiative inherited from Contador development program
rider_signals.ŠTYBAR Zdeněk: positive: heavier rider exploited wet descents by carrying speed through corners, nearly held off group before final sprint
rider_signals.EDET Nicolas: positive: tactical choice to sprint seated in wet gave decisive traction advantage, won from fourth wheel before final turn
rider_signals.VAN DER SANDE Tosh: positive: third place from break sprint, positioning well in technical finale despite conditions
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel: neutral: Astana allowed break to succeed knowing mountain stages ahead more critical for red jersey defense
